USD.catalyst: The U.S. dollar index is holding steady near 100.99, supported by a flight to safety as geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran intensify, driving Brent crude toward the $90 per barrel mark. Market sentiment remains fragile following reports of mutual attacks between the U.S. and Iran, which have pushed average domestic gas prices back to $4 a gallon. While the DXY remains slightly below its 20-day moving average of 101.06, it maintains a structural uptrend above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages. Investors are currently balancing these inflationary energy shocks against a recovery in AI-related equities as they await further clarity on the conflict's impact on global supply chains.

sources.title: Dollar near one-week high as markets grapple with Gulf tensions - CNBC
Dollar firmer as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits $90 - CNBC
Asian shares mostly gain and South Korea and Japan recover some losses from AI stock sell-offs - AP News
Wall Street holds steadier as AI stocks recover some of last week's sharp losses - The Sun Chronicle

GBP.catalyst: Sterling is trading near 1.3431, maintaining a position above its 20, 50, and 200-day moving averages following stronger-than-expected UK GDP data released on July 17. The currency found additional support on reports that Shabana Mahmood will serve as finance minister, with the market reacting positively to the new Treasury leadership despite steady unemployment figures in May. While payrolls declined in June, the pound has held its ground near the 1.3450 level as economists upgrade growth forecasts for the second half of 2026. Traders are now monitoring the impact of the first policy announcements from the new cabinet for further directional cues.

sources.title: Pound Sterling: UK Growth Beats Expectations As Economists Upgrade GDP Outlook - Exchange Rates UK
Breakingviews - New UK Treasury’s big risk is timidity, not daring - Reuters

EUR.catalyst: EUR/USD is trading at 1.1417, pinned near its 20-day moving average as the market prepares for the European Central Bank meeting scheduled for July 23. Policymakers have placed rising oil prices back at the top of their "worry list," potentially complicating the disinflation narrative following last month's energy price retreat. Options markets are currently pricing a Volatility Risk Premium of +2.5%, with implied volatility at 7.1% as strategists eye potential hawkishness to counter Middle East-driven inflationary risks. The pair remains technically heavy, trading below both its 50-day moving average of 1.1526 and its 200-day moving average of 1.1644.

sources.title: Back to square one: Five questions for the ECB - Reuters
Strong Interest in European Residency Event Reflects Global Trends on Saturday 25 July at the Hyatt Regency in Phnom Penh - Cambodia Investment Review

JPY.catalyst: USD/JPY is testing the 162.47 level, its strongest since July 9, as the yen continues to suffer from high energy prices and a dominant +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. Japanese financial authorities have kept markets on edge regarding intervention risk, following estimates that the Ministry of Finance likely spent $34.5 billion in May 2026 to prop up the currency. Despite the spot price holding firmly above the SMA20/50/200 cluster, the options market shows a moderate put skew with a 25-delta Risk Reversal of -1.40, reflecting institutional hedging against another sudden intervention. BoJ holds at 1.0%.

sources.title: JPY/USD: Japan Likely Spent $34.5 Billion in FX Intervention to Boost Yen
JP Morgan stays bearish on the Japanese yen amid higher energy prices
Japan's FX messaging keeps markets on edge over yen intervention risk

AUD.catalyst: AUD/USD is holding at 0.7001, supported by its 20-day moving average at 0.6941 but facing immediate resistance from the 50-day moving average at 0.7046. The pair is benefiting from a broader risk-on flow in Asian equities and an elevated Volatility Risk Premium of +4.9%, suggesting the options market anticipates significant movement relative to recent realized volatility. While technicals suggest a potential move toward 0.7100, the currency remains sensitive to fluctuations in energy costs and regional stability. Market participants are currently monitoring whether the bullish trend above the 100-day moving average can be sustained amid shifting global risk appetites.

CAD.catalyst: The Canadian dollar is trading near 1.4066 against the USD, remaining below its 20-day moving average of 1.4154 after the BoC holds at 2.25%. Governor Tiff Macklem’s team adopted a neutral but cautious stance, predicting the economy will grow by 2.5% in the second quarter as inflation pressures, which recently cooled to 2.8%, begin to ease. The loonie faces headwinds from renewed trade uncertainty as reports emerge of expanded U.S. tariffs beyond USMCA protections. Despite these pressures, the pair remains above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages, reflecting a broader trend of CAD weakness against the greenback.

sources.title: Dollar near one-week high as markets grapple with Gulf tensions - CNBC
Gold holding steady as Bank of Canada leaves rates unchanged - KITCO
Bank of Canada holds rates, says economy is growing again - Reuters

CHF.catalyst: The Swiss franc is trading at 0.8094 against the dollar, positioned above its 20, 50, and 200-day moving averages as safe-haven demand shifts toward the USD. The franc is currently lagging in G10 performance, particularly against the NZD, due to a massive -250bp carry disadvantage with the SNB rate at 0.0% compared to higher-yielding peers. In corporate developments, South Korea’s Samsung Biologics announced an $1.8 billion all-cash bid for Swiss-based PolyPeptide on July 20, a transaction that may influence local liquidity. The currency remains range-bound in the short term as technicals show a "rollercoaster" pattern following recent volatility in global bond yields.

sources.title: Samsung Biologics to launch $1.8 billion all-cash bid for PolyPeptide - Reuters

NZD.catalyst: NZD/USD is trading at 0.5841, buoyed by a significant Q2 CPI beat of 4.1% y/y against the 4.0% expected, which has reinforced the RBNZ’s hawkish policy stance. This data surprise has triggered a squeeze against extreme speculative positioning, where COT data showed leveraged funds were net short -29,582 contracts. The currency is currently the primary beneficiary of G10 divergence, supported by a widening real rate differential and the RBNZ Sectoral Factor Inflation Model holding steady at 2.7%. Technically, the pair has moved above its 20, 50, and 200-day moving averages, signaling a shift in momentum following the July 18 Nations Championship victory which coincided with improved local sentiment.

sources.title: Dollar firmer as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits $90 - CNBC
New Zealand beats Ireland 40-21 to stay unbeaten in the Nations Championship - AP News

NOK.catalyst: The Norwegian krone is trading at 9.667 against the USD, currently positioned below its 20-day moving average of 9.786 and its 200-day moving average of 9.739. The currency remains sensitive to the volatility in Brent crude prices, which have hit $90 amid intensifying Middle East hostilities. A key domestic catalyst is scheduled for July 22, when Norsk Hydro is set to release its second-quarter 2026 results at 07:00 CEST, providing a critical update on Norway's industrial sector performance. Until this data is released, the krone is expected to remain largely driven by global energy headlines and the broader performance of the U.S. dollar.

sources.title: Norsk Hydro: Reminder - Invitation to Hydro's second quarter results 2026 - markets.businessinsider.com

SEK.catalyst: The Swedish krona is trading at 9.673 against the USD, hovering just below its 20-day moving average of 9.679 but remaining well above its 200-day moving average of 9.328. Local market sentiment was recently bolstered by the July 17 Q2 report from industrial group Karnell, which saw EBITA exceed SEK 100 million for the first time, hitting its 15% margin target. Despite these positive microeconomic signals, the krona lacks a clear macroeconomic catalyst as it tracks broader Eurozone sentiment and global risk trends. The currency remains in a consolidative phase pending fresh inflation data or Riksbank commentary to break the current range.

sources.title: Karnell Q2 2026 slides: EBITA tops SEK 100M, hits 15% margin target - Investing.com
